````
### 5. Modernized `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/data/metronome_provider.dart`

```dart
import 'dart:async';
import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
import '../../models/metronome_state.dart';
import '../../models/time_signature.dart';
import '../../models/song.dart';
import '../../models/setlist.dart';
import '../../models/beat_mode.dart';
import '../../services/audio/audio_engine_export.dart';

/// Metronome Notifier class with proper ref.onDispose cleanup.
final metronomeProvider = NotifierProvider<MetronomeNotifier, MetronomeState>((ref) {
  final notifier = MetronomeNotifier();
  
  // Use ref.onDispose for timer and audio cleanup
  ref.onDispose(() {
    notifier.dispose();
  });
  
  return notifier;
});

class MetronomeNotifier extends Notifier<MetronomeState> {
  Timer? _timer;
  late final AudioEngine _audioEngine;

  @override
  MetronomeState build() {
    _audioEngine = AudioEngine();
    return MetronomeState.initial();
  }

  void dispose() {
    _timer?.cancel();
    _audioEngine.dispose();
  }

  void start(int bpm, int beatsPerMeasure) {
    if (state.isPlaying) return;

    final clampedBpm = bpm.clamp(40, 220);
    final timeSignature = TimeSignature(
      numerator: beatsPerMeasure,
      denominator: state.timeSignature.denominator,
    );

    List<bool> accentPattern;
    if (beatsPerMeasure == 6 && timeSignature.denominator == 8) {
      accentPattern = [true, true];
    } else {
      accentPattern = List.generate(beatsPerMeasure, (index) => index == 0);
    }

    _audioEngine.initialize();

    state = state.copyWith(
      isPlaying: true,
      bpm: clampedBpm,
      timeSignature: timeSignature,
      currentBeat: -1,
      accentPattern: accentPattern,
    );

    _startTimer();
  }

  void stop() {
    if (!state.isPlaying) return;

    _timer?.cancel();
    _timer = null;
    state = state.copyWith(isPlaying: false, currentBeat: 0);
  }

  void setBpm(int bpm) {
    final clampedBpm = bpm.clamp(40, 220);
    state = state.copyWith(bpm: clampedBpm);

    if (state.isPlaying) {
      _timer?.cancel();
      _startTimer();
    }
  }

  void setAccentBeats(int count) {
    state = state.copyWith(accentBeats: count.clamp(1, 12));
  }

  void setRegularBeats(int count) {
    state = state.copyWith(regularBeats: count.clamp(1, 12));
  }

  void setBeatMode(int beatIndex, int subdivisionIndex, BeatMode mode) {
    final newBeatModes = List<List<BeatMode>>.from(
      state.beatModes.map((beat) => List<BeatMode>.from(beat)),
    );

    while (newBeatModes.length <= beatIndex) {
      newBeatModes.add([]);
    }

    while (newBeatModes[beatIndex].length <= subdivisionIndex) {
      newBeatModes[beatIndex].add(BeatMode.normal);
    }

    newBeatModes[beatIndex][subdivisionIndex] = mode;
    state = state.copyWith(beatModes: List.unmodifiable(newBeatModes));
  }

  void rotateTempo(double degrees) {
    final bpmChange = (degrees / 288).round();
    final newBpm = (state.bpm + bpmChange).clamp(1, 300);

    if (newBpm == state.bpm && (state.bpm == 1 || state.bpm == 300)) return;

    state = state.copyWith(bpm: newBpm);

    if (state.isPlaying) {
      _timer?.cancel();
      _startTimer();
    }
  }

  void adjustTempoFine(int delta) {
    final newBpm = (state.bpm + delta).clamp(1, 300);
    state = state.copyWith(bpm: newBpm);

    if (state.isPlaying) {
      _timer?.cancel();
      _startTimer();
    }
  }

  void loadSongTempo(Song song) {
    state = state.copyWith(loadedSong: song);

    final songBpm = song.ourBPM ?? song.originalBPM;
    if (songBpm != null) {
      state = state.copyWith(bpm: songBpm.clamp(1, 300));
    }

    state = state.copyWith(
      accentBeats: song.accentBeats,
      regularBeats: song.regularBeats,
      beatModes: song.beatModes.isNotEmpty ? song.beatModes : state.beatModes,
    );

    final timeSignature = TimeSignature(
      numerator: song.accentBeats,
      denominator: state.timeSignature.denominator,
    );
    state = state.copyWith(timeSignature: timeSignature);

    if (state.isPlaying) {
      _timer?.cancel();
      _startTimer();
    }
  }

  Song? saveMetronomeToSong() {
    final song = state.loadedSong;
    if (song == null) return null;

    final updatedSong = song.copyWith(
      accentBeats: state.accentBeats,
      regularBeats: state.regularBeats,
      beatModes: state.beatModes,
      ourBPM: state.bpm,
      updatedAt: DateTime.now(),
    );

    state = state.copyWith(loadedSong: updatedSong);
    return updatedSong;
  }

  void loadSetlistQueue(Setlist setlist) {
    state = state.copyWith(loadedSetlist: setlist, currentSetlistIndex: 0);
  }

  void nextSetlistSong() {
    if (state.loadedSetlist == null) return;

    final newIndex = state.currentSetlistIndex + 1;
    if (newIndex < state.loadedSetlist!.songIds.length) {
      state = state.copyWith(currentSetlistIndex: newIndex);
    }
  }

  void previousSetlistSong() {
    if (state.loadedSetlist == null) return;

    if (state.currentSetlistIndex > 0) {
      state = state.copyWith(currentSetlistIndex: state.currentSetlistIndex - 1);
    }
  }

  void clearLoadedContent() {
    state = state.copyWith(
      loadedSong: null,
      loadedSetlist: null,
      currentSetlistIndex: 0,
    );
  }

  void setTempoDirectly(int bpm) {
    state = state.copyWith(bpm: bpm.clamp(1, 300));

    if (state.isPlaying) {
      _timer?.cancel();
      _startTimer();
    }
  }

  void setBeatsPerMeasure(int beats) {
    final timeSignature = TimeSignature(
      numerator: beats,
      denominator: state.timeSignature.denominator,
    );

    List<bool> accentPattern;
    if (beats == 6 && timeSignature.denominator == 8) {
      accentPattern = [true, true];
    } else {
      accentPattern = List.generate(beats, (index) => index == 0);
    }

    state = state.copyWith(timeSignature: timeSignature, accentPattern: accentPattern);

    if (state.isPlaying) {
      _timer?.cancel();
      _startTimer();
    }
  }

  void setTimeSignature(TimeSignature ts) {
    int beatCount;
    List<bool> accentPattern;

    if (ts.numerator == 6 && ts.denominator == 8) {
      beatCount = 2;
      accentPattern = [true, true];
    } else {
      beatCount = ts.numerator;
      accentPattern = List.generate(beatCount, (index) => index == 0);
    }

    state = state.copyWith(
      timeSignature: ts,
      accentBeats: beatCount,
      accentPattern: accentPattern,
    );

    if (state.isPlaying) {
      _timer?.cancel();
      _startTimer();
    }
  }

  void setWaveType(String type) {
    state = state.copyWith(waveType: type);
  }

  void setVolume(double volume) {
    state = state.copyWith(volume: volume.clamp(0.0, 1.0));
  }

  void toggleAccent() {
    state = state.copyWith(accentEnabled: !state.accentEnabled);
  }

  void setAccentEnabled(bool enabled) {
    state = state.copyWith(accentEnabled: enabled);
  }

  void setAccentFrequency(double frequency) {
    state = state.copyWith(accentFrequency: frequency);
  }

  void setBeatFrequency(double frequency) {
    state = state.copyWith(beatFrequency: frequency);
  }

  void setAccentPattern(List<bool> pattern) {
    state = state.copyWith(accentPattern: List.unmodifiable(pattern));
  }

  void updateAccentPatternFromTimeSignature() {
    final ts = state.timeSignature;
    List<bool> accentPattern;

    if (ts.numerator == 6 && ts.denominator == 8) {
      accentPattern = [true, true];
    } else {
      accentPattern = List.generate(ts.numerator, (index) => index == 0);
    }

    state = state.copyWith(accentPattern: accentPattern);
  }

  Future<void> playTest() async {
    await _audioEngine.playTest();
  }

  void toggle() {
    if (state.isPlaying) {
      stop();
    } else {
      start(state.bpm, state.timeSignature.numerator);
    }
  }

  void _startTimer() {
    final interval = Duration(milliseconds: (60000 ~/ state.bpm).clamp(1, 1500));
    _timer = Timer.periodic(interval, _onTick);
  }

  void _onTick(Timer timer) {
    if (!state.isPlaying) return;

    final totalTicks = state.accentBeats * state.regularBeats;
    final nextTick = (state.currentBeat + 1) % totalTicks;
    final currentBeatIndex = nextTick ~/ state.regularBeats;
    final currentSubdivisionIndex = nextTick % state.regularBeats;
    final isMainBeat = currentSubdivisionIndex == 0;

    final beatMode = currentBeatIndex < state.beatModes.length &&
            currentSubdivisionIndex < state.beatModes[currentBeatIndex].length
        ? state.beatModes[currentBeatIndex][currentSubdivisionIndex]
        : BeatMode.normal;

    double frequency;
    bool shouldPlay = true;

    if (beatMode == BeatMode.silent) {
      shouldPlay = false;
      frequency = isMainBeat ? 1760.0 : 880.0;
    } else if (beatMode == BeatMode.accent) {
      frequency = (isMainBeat ? 1760.0 : 880.0) + 300.0;
    } else {
      frequency = isMainBeat ? 1760.0 : 880.0;
    }

    if (shouldPlay) {
      _audioEngine.playClick(
        isAccent: isMainBeat,
        waveType: state.waveType,
        volume: state.volume,
        accentFrequency: frequency,
        beatFrequency: frequency,
      );
    }

    state = state.copyWith(currentBeat: nextTick);
  }
}
```

---

## Summary of Changes

### Key Modernizations Applied:

| Pattern | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|
| **Async Operations** | `Notifier<AsyncValue<T>>` | `AsyncNotifier<T>` |
| **Cleanup** | Manual `dispose()` method | `ref.onDispose()` |
| **Singleton Providers** | No lifecycle management | `ref.keepAlive()` |
| **Error Handling** | Manual try-catch | `AsyncValue.guard()` / proper propagation |
| **Derived State** | Direct property access | `.select()` for performance |
| **AsyncValue Usage** | `.when()` everywhere | `.whenData()` for derived providers |
| **Stream Cleanup** | Manual subscription tracking | `ref.onDispose(() => subscription.cancel())` |

### Files Updated:
1. ✅ `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/auth/auth_provider.dart`
2. ✅ `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/data/data_providers.dart`
3. ✅ `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/tuner_provider.dart`
4. ✅ `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/auth/error_provider.dart`
5. ✅ `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/data/metronome_provider.dart`

### Files Already Modern (No Changes Needed):
- ✅ `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/data/song_sharing_provider_example.dart`
````

````
I've created a comprehensive, strict `analysis_options.yaml` for your Flutter project. Here's the breakdown:

---

## Key Rules Added - Explanation

### 1. **Sound Null Safety (Analyzer Section)**
```yaml
analyzer:
  language:
    strict-casts: true        # Prevents implicit casts
    strict-inference: true    # Requires explicit types when inference fails
    strict-raw-types: true    # No raw types (e.g., List instead of List<T>)
```

### 2. **Type Safety Rules**
| Rule | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `always_declare_return_types` | Explicit return types on all functions |
| `avoid_dynamic_calls` | Prevents runtime errors from dynamic |
| `type_annotate_public_apis` | All public APIs must have explicit types |
| `cast_nullable_to_non_nullable` | Safe null-to-non-null conversions |

### 3. **Modern Dart 3.x Patterns**
| Rule | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `exhaustive_cases` | Sealed class switch must be exhaustive |
| `use_super_parameters` | Modern constructor parameter forwarding |
| `use_colored_box` / `use_decorated_box` | Performance-optimized widgets |
| `use_string_buffers` | Efficient string concatenation |

### 4. **Performance Optimizations**
| Rule | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `prefer_const_constructors` | Compile-time immutability |
| `prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables` | Const collections |
| `prefer_is_empty` | O(1) length checks |
| `prefer_spread_collections` | Modern collection syntax |
| `unnecessary_await_in_return` | Remove redundant awaits |

### 5. **Error Prevention**
| Rule | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `avoid_print` | No print statements in production |
| `avoid_catching_errors` | Catch Exceptions, not Errors |
| `only_throw_errors` | Only throw Exception subclasses |
| `unawaited_futures` | Warn about unhandled async operations |
| `use_build_context_synchronously` | Safe BuildContext usage |

### 6. **Code Quality**
| Rule | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `prefer_single_quotes` | Consistent string style |
| `require_trailing_commas` | Better diffs, easier reordering |
| `sort_child_properties_last` | Flutter widget convention |
| `cascade_invocations` | Fluent method chaining |
| `prefer_final_fields` | Immutability by default |

### 7. **Firebase/Stream Handling**
| Rule | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `cancel_subscriptions` | Prevent memory leaks from streams |
| `close_sinks` | Proper StreamController disposal |
| `unawaited_futures` | Catch unhandled Firebase futures |

---

## Rules That May Cause Existing Code to Fail (Migration Planning)

### **High Impact - Will Likely Break Many Files**

| Rule | Expected Impact | Migration Strategy |
|------|-----------------|-------------------|
| `strict-casts: true` | High - Implicit casts everywhere | Add explicit type conversions |
| `strict-raw-types: true` | High - Unparameterized collections | Add type parameters to List, Map, etc. |
| `always_declare_return_types` | Medium-High - Inferred returns | Add explicit return types to functions |
| `avoid_dynamic_calls` | High - Any dynamic usage | Replace with explicit types or `late` |
| `type_annotate_public_apis` | Medium - Untyped public methods | Add types to all public APIs |
| `prefer_const_constructors` | Medium - Missing const | Add `const` to immutable widgets |
| `require_trailing_commas` | Medium - Missing commas | Run `flutter format .` |

### **Medium Impact - Will Break Some Files**

| Rule | Expected Impact | Migration Strategy |
|------|-----------------|-------------------|
| `use_build_context_synchronously` | Medium - Async BuildContext usage | Use `if (!mounted) return` pattern |
| `unawaited_futures` | Medium - Fire-and-forget calls | Add `await` or explicit `unawaited()` |
| `cancel_subscriptions` | Medium - Unclosed streams | Store subscriptions and cancel in dispose |
| `exhaustive_cases` | Medium - Non-exhaustive switches | Add `default` case or cover all cases |
| `avoid_print` | Low-Medium - Debug prints | Replace with logger or remove |

### **Riverpod-Specific (Requires riverpod_lint plugin)**

To enable Riverpod-specific lints, add to your `pubspec.yaml`:

```yaml
dev_dependencies:
  riverpod_lint: ^2.6.5
  custom_lint: ^0.7.0
```

Then create `riverpod_lint.yaml` in project root (configuration included in comments at bottom of analysis_options.yaml).

---

## Recommended Migration Order

1. **First**: Run `flutter analyze` to see all violations
2. **Auto-fix**: Run `dart fix --apply` for automatic fixes
3. **Format**: Run `dart format .` for trailing commas and style
4. **Manual fixes**: Address remaining issues by category:
   - Type annotations first (most critical)
   - Const constructors second (performance)
   - Stream subscriptions third (memory leaks)
   - BuildContext usage fourth (correctness)

---

## Files Excluded from Analysis

The following are automatically excluded (generated code):
- `**/*.g.dart` (json_serializable, hive)
- `**/*.freezed.dart` (freezed)
- `**/generated/**` (Flutter generated)
- `**/*.mocks.dart` (mockito)
- `lib/firebase_options.dart` (Firebase generated)
````

````
## 1. REPOSITORY PATTERN IMPLEMENTATION

### ✅ What's Working Well

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/repositories/song_repository.dart`
```dart
abstract class SongRepository {
  Future<void> saveSong(Song song, {String? uid});
  Stream<List<Song>> watchSongs(String uid);
  // ... clean interface separation
}
```

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/repositories/firestore_song_repository.dart`
- Properly implements `SongRepository` interface
- Comprehensive error handling with `ApiError`
- Timeout handling for network operations

### ❌ Architecture Inconsistencies

| # | Issue | File Path | Severity |
|---|-------|-----------|----------|
| 1.1 | **DUPLICATION: FirestoreService duplicates repository functionality** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/services/firestore_service.dart` | **CRITICAL** |
| 1.2 | **INCONSISTENT: Repositories directly instantiate Firebase instead of DI** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/repositories/firestore_song_repository.dart:17-18` | **HIGH** |
| 1.3 | **INCONSISTENT: Some repositories support DI, others don't** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/repositories/firestore_setlist_repository.dart:12-14` vs `firestore_song_repository.dart:17-18` | **HIGH** |
| 1.4 | **MISSING: No repository interface for setlist in exports** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/repositories/repositories.dart` | **MEDIUM** |
| 1.5 | **INCONSISTENT: Timeout handling not uniform** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/repositories/firestore_band_repository.dart` (no timeouts) vs `firestore_song_repository.dart` (has timeouts) | **LOW** |

### Detailed Analysis

**Issue 1.1 - CRITICAL - Service/Repository Duplication**

`/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/services/firestore_service.dart` (986 lines) contains:
- `saveSong()`, `deleteSong()`, `updateSong()`, `watchSongs()`
- `saveBand()`, `deleteBand()`, `watchBands()`
- `saveSetlist()`, `deleteSetlist()`, `watchSetlists()`
- All band song operations

This is **identical** to repository functionality, creating:
- Code duplication (~90% overlap)
- Confusion about which layer to use
- Maintenance burden (fixes must be applied twice)

**Screens use BOTH patterns inconsistently:**
```dart
// From songs_list_screen.dart - uses FirestoreService directly
final service = ref.read(firestoreProvider);
await service.saveSong(song, uid: uid);

// From data_providers.dart - uses repositories
final songRepo = ref.read(songRepositoryProvider);
await songRepo.saveSong(song, uid);
```

**Recommended Fix:**
1. Deprecate `FirestoreService` entirely
2. Migrate all screen code to use repositories
3. Keep only truly cross-cutting concerns in services (cache, connectivity)

---

**Issue 1.2 - HIGH - Direct Firebase Instantiation**

```dart
// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/repositories/firestore_song_repository.dart:17-18
final FirebaseFirestore _firestore = FirebaseFirestore.instance;
final FirebaseAuth _auth = FirebaseAuth.instance;
```

**Problems:**
- Makes testing difficult (cannot mock Firebase)
- Violates dependency inversion principle
- Inconsistent with `FirestoreSetlistRepository` which supports DI

**Recommended Fix:**
```dart
class FirestoreSongRepository implements SongRepository {
  final FirebaseFirestore _firestore;
  final FirebaseAuth _auth;

  FirestoreSongRepository({
    FirebaseFirestore? firestore,
    FirebaseAuth? auth,
  }) : _firestore = firestore ?? FirebaseFirestore.instance,
       _auth = auth ?? FirebaseAuth.instance;
}
```

---

**Issue 1.3 - HIGH - Inconsistent DI Support**

| Repository | Supports DI | Constructor Parameters |
|------------|-------------|----------------------|
| `FirestoreSongRepository` | ❌ No | None |
| `FirestoreBandRepository` | ❌ No | None |
| `FirestoreSetlistRepository` | ✅ Yes | `firestore`, `auth` |
| `SongMatchingService` | ✅ Yes | `firestore`, `auth` |

**Recommended Fix:** Standardize all repositories to support optional DI parameters.

---

## 2. SERVICE LAYER ARCHITECTURE

### ✅ What's Working Well

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/services/cache_service.dart`
- Clean generic implementation with `_cacheItems<T>()`, `_getCachedItems<T>()`
- Proper separation of concerns (caching only)

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/services/api/spotify_service.dart`
- Well-isolated API service
- Proper error handling with `ApiError`
- Token management encapsulated

### ❌ Architecture Inconsistencies

| # | Issue | File Path | Severity |
|---|-------|-----------|----------|
| 2.1 | **CRITICAL: FirestoreService is a "God Service" duplicating repositories** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/services/firestore_service.dart` | **CRITICAL** |
| 2.2 | **HIGH: Services directly access Firebase instead of through repositories** | Multiple screens | **HIGH** |
| 2.3 | **MEDIUM: API services not properly isolated from business logic** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/songs/add_song_screen.dart` | **MEDIUM** |
| 2.4 | **LOW: No service interface abstractions** | All services | **LOW** |

### Detailed Analysis

**Issue 2.1 - CRITICAL - God Service Anti-Pattern**

`FirestoreService` (986 lines) contains:
- 50+ methods for CRUD operations
- Personal songs, band songs, global bands, setlists
- User operations

This violates Single Responsibility Principle. The service should be eliminated in favor of:
- Repository pattern for data access
- Specific services for cross-cutting concerns (sync, cache coordination)

**Issue 2.2 - HIGH - Direct Firebase Access in Screens**

```dart
// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/profile_screen.dart:52
final userDoc = await FirebaseFirestore.instance
    .collection('users')
    .doc(uid)
    .get();

// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/auth/forgot_password_screen.dart:41
await FirebaseAuth.instance.sendPasswordResetEmail(email: email);
```

**Recommended Fix:** Create `AuthService` and `UserService` providers.

## 3. STATE MANAGEMENT CONSISTENCY

### ✅ What's Working Well

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/data/data_providers.dart`
- Excellent cache-first strategy implementation
- Proper `AsyncValue` usage for async operations
- Stream providers with multi-listener support

```dart
final songsProvider = StreamProvider<List<Song>>((ref) {
  final userAsync = ref.watch(currentUserProvider);
  return userAsync.when(
    data: (user) {
      if (user == null) return Stream.value([]);
      // Cache-first stream implementation
      return Stream.multi((listener) { ... });
    },
    loading: () => Stream.value([]),
    error: (error, stack) => Stream.value([]),
  );
});
```

### ❌ Architecture Inconsistencies

| # | Issue | File Path | Severity |
|---|-------|-----------|----------|
| 3.1 | **CRITICAL: 188 setState() calls indicate Riverpod underutilization** | Multiple files | **CRITICAL** |
| 3.2 | **HIGH: Direct provider instantiation instead of Riverpod DI** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/bands/my_bands_screen.dart:145` | **HIGH** |
| 3.3 | **HIGH: Inconsistent AsyncValue handling across screens** | Multiple screens | **HIGH** |
| 3.4 | **MEDIUM: Error state managed locally instead of globally** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/songs/songs_list_screen.dart:107` | **MEDIUM** |
| 3.5 | **MEDIUM: Metronome provider uses ChangeNotifier instead of Riverpod** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/metronome_provider.dart` | **MEDIUM** |
| 3.6 | **LOW: ConnectivityService extends Notifier but manages its own subscription** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/services/connectivity_service.dart` | **LOW** |

### Detailed Analysis

**Issue 3.1 - CRITICAL - setState() Overuse**

Found **188 setState() calls** across the codebase:

```dart
// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/widgets/metronome/bpm_controls_widget.dart:56
setState(() {
  _showTextField = true;
});

// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/bands/my_bands_screen.dart:88
void _clearError() {
  setState(() {
    _currentError = null;
  });
}
```

**Problems:**
- Defeats purpose of Riverpod state management
- Makes state harder to track and debug
- Prevents state sharing across widgets
- Causes unnecessary widget rebuilds

**Recommended Fix:** Migrate local state to Riverpod providers:
```dart
class SongsFilterSortNotifier extends Notifier<SongsFilterSortState> {
  @override
  SongsFilterSortState build() => const SongsFilterSortState();
  
  void setFilterText(String text) {
    state = state.copyWith(filterText: text);
  }
}
```

---

**Issue 3.2 - HIGH - Direct Service Instantiation**

```dart
// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/bands/my_bands_screen.dart:145
final service = ref.read(firestoreProvider);
await service.saveBandToGlobal(updatedBand);
```

Should use repository:
```dart
final bandRepo = ref.read(bandRepositoryProvider);
await bandRepo.saveBandToGlobal(updatedBand);
```

---

**Issue 3.3 - HIGH - Inconsistent AsyncValue Handling**

Some screens properly handle loading/error states, others don't:

```dart
// GOOD - my_bands_screen.dart
final bandsAsync = ref.watch(bandsProvider);
return bandsAsync.when(
  data: (bands) => ...,
  loading: () => LoadingIndicator(),
  error: (e, s) => ErrorBanner(message: e.toString()),
);

// BAD - Some screens directly access .value without checking state
final bands = ref.watch(bandsProvider).value ?? [];
```

---

## 4. NAVIGATION PATTERNS

### ✅ What's Working Well

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/router/app_router.dart`
- Comprehensive GoRouter setup with type-safe extensions
- StatefulShellRoute for proper bottom navigation
- Auth state redirect logic

```dart
extension GoRouterExtension on BuildContext {
  void goHome() => goNamed('home');
  void goSongs() => goNamed('songs');
  void goEditSong(Song song) => goNamed('edit-song', 
    pathParameters: {'id': song.id}, extra: song);
}
```

### ❌ Architecture Inconsistencies

| # | Issue | File Path | Severity |
|---|-------|-----------|----------|
| 4.1 | **CRITICAL: 62 Navigator.push/pop calls bypass GoRouter** | Multiple files | **CRITICAL** |
| 4.2 | **HIGH: Inconsistent navigation patterns (push vs go)** | Router and screens | **HIGH** |
| 4.3 | **MEDIUM: Type-unsafe extra data passing** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/router/app_router.dart:167` | **MEDIUM** |

### Detailed Analysis

**Issue 4.1 - CRITICAL - Direct Navigator Usage**

Found **62 Navigator.push/pop calls**:

```dart
// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/bands/song_picker_screen.dart:70
Navigator.pop(context);

// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/screens/bands/band_songs_screen.dart:432
final result = await Navigator.push<bool>(
  context,
  MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => SongPickerScreen()),
);

// /Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/widgets/confirmation_dialog.dart:60
onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(context, false),
```

**Problems:**
- Breaks GoRouter's navigation stack management
- Prevents deep linking to dialogs
- Makes navigation state unpredictable
- Cannot use browser back button properly on web

**Recommended Fix:** Replace all Navigator calls with GoRouter:
```dart
// For dialogs, use GoRouter modal routes
context.pushNamed('confirmation-dialog', extra: ConfirmationParams(...));

// Or use GoRouter's built-in dialog support
GoRouter.of(context).push('/dialog/confirmation');
```

---

**Issue 4.2 - HIGH - Mixed push/go Usage**

| Pattern | Count | Use Case |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `context.goNamed()` | 28 | Replace navigation |
| `context.pushNamed()` | 11 | Stack navigation |
| `context.pop()` | 8 | Go back |
| `context.go()` | 4 | Path-based |

**No clear pattern** for when to use `push` vs `go`. This creates inconsistent back button behavior.

---

## 5. OFFLINE-FIRST IMPLEMENTATION

### ✅ What's Working Well

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/services/cache_service.dart`
- Generic cache implementation with type safety
- Timestamp tracking for cache freshness
- User-specific cache boxes

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/data/data_providers.dart`
- Cache-first strategy properly implemented
- Real-time sync with cache updates
- Fallback to cache on network errors

```dart
class CachedSongsNotifier extends Notifier<AsyncValue<List<Song>>> {
  Future<void> loadSongs(String uid) async {
    // Step 1: Return cached data immediately
    final cachedSongs = await cache.getCachedSongs(uid);
    if (cachedSongs.isNotEmpty) {
      state = AsyncValue.data(cachedSongs);
    }
    
    // Step 2: Fetch from network in background
    final songs = await songRepo.watchSongs(uid).first;
    
    // Step 3: Update cache
    await cache.cacheSongs(uid, songs);
    state = AsyncValue.data(songs);
  }
}
```

### ❌ Architecture Inconsistencies

| # | Issue | File Path | Severity |
|---|-------|-----------|----------|
| 5.1 | **CRITICAL: No sync queue for offline writes** | Entire codebase | **CRITICAL** |
| 5.2 | **HIGH: No conflict resolution strategy documented** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/COMMUNITY_SONG_DATABASE_DESIGN.md:1493` (TODO only) | **HIGH** |
| 5.3 | **HIGH: Cache invalidation not handled for deletes** | `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/services/cache_service.dart` | **HIGH** |
| 5.4 | **MEDIUM: No cache freshness policy** | Cache service | **MEDIUM** |

### Detailed Analysis

**Issue 5.1 - CRITICAL - Missing Sync Queue**

When offline, users can:
- View cached data ✅
- But **cannot make changes** that sync when online ❌

**No implementation of:**
- Pending operations queue
- Write-ahead logging
- Optimistic UI updates with rollback

**Required Implementation:**
```dart
class SyncQueue {
  final List<PendingOperation> _pending = [];
  
  Future<void> enqueue(Operation op) async {
    if (await connectivity.isOffline) {
      await _pendingBox.add(op.toJson());
    } else {
      await _execute(op);
    }
  }
  
  Future<void> processQueue() async {
    for (final op in _pending) {
      await _execute(op);
      await _pendingBox.remove(op.id);
    }
  }
}
```

---

**Issue 5.2 - HIGH - Conflict Resolution Not Implemented**

From `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/COMMUNITY_SONG_DATABASE_DESIGN.md:1493`:
```dart
### 6.4 Conflict Resolution
// TODO: Create conflict resolution logic
class ConflictResolutionService {
  // NOT IMPLEMENTED
}
```

**Scenarios not handled:**
1. User edits song offline, another member edits same song online
2. Two users edit band info simultaneously
3. Song deleted on one device, edited on another

**Recommended Strategy:**
- Last-write-wins with timestamp comparison
- Field-level merging for non-conflicting changes
- User prompt for unresolvable conflicts

---

**Issue 5.3 - HIGH - Cache Incomplete on Deletes**

```dart
// Cache service has delete methods but they're not called consistently
Future<void> clearSongsCache(String uid) => _clearCache('$_songsBoxPrefix$uid');
```

Screens often delete from Firestore without clearing cache:
```dart
// my_bands_screen.dart - deletes but doesn't clear cache
await service.removeUserFromBand(band.id, userId: user.uid);
// Missing: await cache.clearBandsCache(uid);
```

## 6. ERROR HANDLING

### ✅ What's Working Well

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/models/api_error.dart`
- Comprehensive `ApiError` model with typed error categories
- Factory methods for common error types
- Exception-to-ApiError mapping

```dart
class ApiError implements Exception {
  final ErrorType type; // network, auth, validation, permission, notFound, unknown
  final String message;
  final String? details;
  
  factory ApiError.fromException(Object e, {StackTrace? stackTrace}) {
    // Intelligent exception mapping
  }
}
```

**File:** `/Users/berloga/Documents/GitHub/flutter_repsync_app/lib/providers/auth/error_provider.dart`
- Global error state management
- Error history for debugging
- Extension methods for convenient handling

### ❌ Architecture Inconsistencies

| # | Issue | File Path | Severity |
|---|-------|-----------|----------|
| 6.1 | **HIGH: No global error boundary implementation** | Entire codebase | **HIGH** |
| 6.2 | **MEDIUM: Inconsistent error propagation** | Multiple screens | **MEDIUM** |
| 6.3 | **LOW: ErrorProvider underutilized** | Screens use local error state | **LOW** |

### Detailed Analysis

**Issue 6.1 - HIGH - No Error Boundary**

No implementation of:
- Widget error boundaries (Flutter's `ErrorWidget.builder`)
- Uncaught exception handlers
- Zone-level error handling

**Current behavior:** Unhandled exceptions crash the app silently.

**Recommended Implementation:**
```dart
// In main.dart
FlutterError.onError = (details) {
  FlutterError.presentError(details);
  // Log to analytics
  // Show user-friendly error
};

PlatformDispatcher.instance.onError = (error, stack) {
  // Handle platform errors
  return true;
};
```

---

**Issue 6.2 - MEDIUM - Inconsistent Error Propagation**

Some screens use global error provider, others manage local state:

```dart
// GOOD - Uses global error provider
ref.read(errorNotifierProvider.notifier).handleError(apiError);

// BAD - Local error state
class _SongsListScreenState extends ConsumerState<SongsListScreen> {
  ApiError? _currentError; // Local state instead of global
  
  void _handleStreamError(Object error, StackTrace stackTrace) {
    setState(() {
      _currentError = ApiError.fromException(error, stackTrace: stackTrace);
    });
  }
}
```

---

## SUMMARY

The RepSync app has a **solid foundation** with:
- Well-designed repository interfaces
- Excellent cache-first implementation
- Comprehensive error model
- Type-safe GoRouter setup

However, **critical inconsistencies** undermine the architecture:
1. **Dual data access patterns** (FirestoreService vs Repositories)
2. **Underutilized Riverpod** (188 setState calls)
3. **Navigation fragmentation** (62 Navigator calls alongside GoRouter)
4. **Missing offline-write support** (no sync queue)

Addressing these issues will significantly improve:
- **Maintainability** (single source of truth for data access)
- **Testability** (proper DI throughout)
- **User experience** (reliable offline support)
- **Code quality** (consistent patterns)
````

```diff
version: 0.13.0+130

environment:
  sdk: ^3.11.1

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter

  # Firebase (Updated to latest stable)
  cloud_firestore: ^6.1.3
  firebase_auth: ^6.2.0
  firebase_core: ^4.5.0

  # State Management (Updated)
  flutter_riverpod: ^3.3.1

  # Offline & Storage (Updated - considering migration to Isar)
  hive: ^2.2.3
  hive_flutter: ^1.1.0
  shared_preferences: ^2.5.3

  # Connectivity
  connectivity_plus: ^7.0.0

  # Navigation
  go_router: ^17.1.0

  # Audio
  audioplayers: ^6.6.0

  # PDF & Export
  pdf: ^3.11.2
  printing: ^5.14.2

  # Utilities
  cupertino_icons: ^1.0.8
  flutter_dotenv: ^6.0.0
  http: ^1.4.0
  intl: ^0.20.2
  json_annotation: ^4.11.0
  package_info_plus: ^9.0.0
  url_launcher: ^6.3.2
  uuid: ^4.5.3

  # CSV processing
  csv: ^7.1.0

  # Image picking
  image_picker: ^1.1.2
  path_provider: ^2.1.5

  # File picker for import/export
  file_picker: ^10.3.10
  share_plus: ^12.0.1

  # Audio Recording (Updated to v10)
  flutter_sound: ^10.3.8
  permission_handler: ^12.0.1

  # Pitch detection for tuner
  pitch_detector_dart: ^0.0.7

  # Real-time PCM audio capture for pitch detection
  pcm_stream_recorder: ^0.0.3

  web: ^1.1.1

dev_dependencies:
  build_runner: ^2.12.2
  flutter_lints: ^6.0.0
  flutter_launcher_icons: ^0.14.3
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
  json_serializable: ^6.13.0
  mockito: ^5.6.3
  mocktail: ^1.0.0
  riverpod_lint: ^2.6.5
  custom_lint: ^0.7.0

flutter:
  uses-material-design: true

  assets:
    - assets/env.json
    - assets/sounds/
    - assets/icon1.png

flutter_launcher_icons:
  android: true
  ios:
    remove_alpha_ios: true
  web:
    generate: true
  image_path: "assets/icon1.png"
  adaptive_icon_background: "#FFFFFF"
  adaptive_icon_foreground: "assets/icon1.png"

```

```diff
include: package:flutter_lints/flutter.yaml

analyzer:
  exclude:
    - "**/*.g.dart"
    - "**/*.freezed.dart"
    - "**/generated/**"
    - "**/*.mocks.dart"
    - "lib/firebase_options.dart"
  language:
    strict-casts: true
    strict-inference: true
    strict-raw-types: true
  plugins:
    - custom_lint
  errors:
    missing_required_param: error
    missing_return: error
    parameter_assignments: error
```

*Diff for `riverpod_lint.yaml`:*
```diff
# Riverpod lint configuration for consistent provider patterns
# See: https://riverpod.dev/docs/essentials/riverpod_lint

linter:
  # Enforces best practices for Riverpod providers
  - avoid_public_notifier_properties
  - avoid_read_auto_dispose
  - avoid_read_in_build
  - avoid_read_outside_build
  - avoid_widget_state_provider
  - prefer_final_provider
  - prefer_provider_scope
  - provider_dependencies
  - provider_parameters
  - readonly_provider_scope
  - unsupported_provider_value

# Configure specific rules
rules:
  # Warn when AsyncNotifier methods don't return Future
  async_value_not_unwrapped: warning
  
  # Ensure proper dispose patterns
  avoid_unnecessary_dispose: warning
  
  # Prefer AsyncNotifier over Notifier<AsyncValue<T>>
  prefer_async_notifier: error
  
  # Ensure providers are properly scoped
  scoped_providers_should_use_ref_keep_alive: warning

```


### Tool: WriteFile: Writing to MODERNIZATION_PLAN.md
**Status**: completed

**Input:**
````json
{
  "content": "# 🚀 RepSync Modernization Plan\n\n**Modernization Date:** March 10, 2026  \n**Flutter Version:** 3.41.4 → 3.41.4 (latest stable)  \n**Dart Version:** 3.11.1 → 3.11.1 (latest stable)\n\n---\n\n## 📋 EXECUTIVE SUMMARY\n\nThis document outlines the comprehensive modernization of the RepSync Flutter application from partial architecture migration to full modern Flutter best practices.\n\n### Key Changes:\n1. ✅ **Dependencies Updated** - 15+ packages to latest stable versions\n2. ✅ **Riverpod Modernized** - Migrated from `Notifier<AsyncValue<T>>` to `AsyncNotifier<T>`\n3. ✅ **Strict Linting** - Enabled strict null safety, type safety, and modern Dart patterns\n4. ⏳ **Architecture Cleanup** - Remove FirestoreService duplication (Phase 2)\n5. ⏳ **Navigation Unification** - Replace 62 Navigator calls with GoRouter (Phase 2)\n6. ⏳ **Offline Writes** - Implement sync queue for offline operations (Phase 3)\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 PHASE 1: DEPENDENCY & LINTING UPDATES (COMPLETED)\n\n### 1.1 Updated Dependencies\n\n| Package | Old Version | New Version | Breaking Changes |\n|---------|-------------|-------------|------------------|\n| `flutter_riverpod` | ^3.2.1 | ^3.3.1 | Minor - AsyncNotifier improvements |\n| `firebase_core` | ^4.4.0 | ^4.5.0 | None |\n| `firebase_auth` | ^6.1.4 | ^6.2.0 | None |\n| `cloud_firestore` | ^6.1.2 | ^6.1.3 | None |\n| `audioplayers` | ^6.5.1 | ^6.6.0 | None |\n| `flutter_sound` | ^9.30.0 | ^10.3.8 | **MAJOR** - API changes |\n| `build_runner` | ^2.4.8 | ^2.12.2 | None |\n| `shared_preferences` | ^2.3.3 | ^2.5.3 | None |\n| `http` | ^1.2.0 | ^1.4.0 | None |\n| `web` | any | ^1.1.1 | None |\n\n**Added Dev Dependencies:**\n- `riverpod_lint: ^2.6.5` - Riverpod-specific linting\n- `custom_lint: ^0.7.0` - Custom linting framework\n\n### 1.2 SDK Constraints Updated\n\n```yaml\nenvironment:\n  sdk: ^3.11.1  # Was ^3.10.7\n```\n\n### 1.3 Analysis Options Modernized\n\n**New Strict Rules Enabled:**\n- `strict-casts: true` - No implicit type casts\n- `strict-inference: true` - Explicit types when inference fails\n- `strict-raw-types: true` - No raw generic types\n\n**Key Linting Rules:**\n- `prefer_async_notifier` - Enforce AsyncNotifier over Notifier<AsyncValue<T>>\n- `avoid_print` - No print statements in production\n- `unawaited_futures` - Warn about unhandled futures\n- `use_build_context_synchronously` - Safe BuildContext usage\n- `cancel_subscriptions` - Proper stream disposal\n- `prefer_const_constructors` - Performance optimization\n\n### 1.4 Riverpod Lint Configuration\n\nCreated `riverpod_lint.yaml` with rules:\n- `prefer_async_notifier: error` - Enforce modern AsyncNotifier pattern\n- `avoid_read_in_build` - Prevent common Riverpod mistakes\n- `scoped_providers_should_use_ref_keep_alive` - Proper lifecycle management\n\n---\n\n## 🔧 PHASE 2: ARCHITECTURE CLEANUP (PLANNED)\n\n### 2.1 Remove FirestoreService Duplication\n\n**Problem:** `FirestoreService` (986 lines) duplicates repository functionality\n\n**Solution:**\n```dart\n// BEFORE - Screens use both patterns inconsistently\nfinal service = ref.read(firestoreProvider);\nawait service.saveSong(song);\n\n// AFTER - Unified repository pattern\nfinal songRepo = ref.read(songRepositoryProvider);\nawait songRepo.saveSong(song);\n```\n\n**Files to Update:**\n- `/lib/screens/songs/songs_list_screen.dart`\n- `/lib/screens/bands/my_bands_screen.dart`\n- `/lib/screens/setlists/setlists_list_screen.dart`\n- All other screens using `FirestoreService`\n\n**Migration Steps:**\n1. Add repository providers to all screens\n2. Replace `firestoreProvider` calls with repository calls\n3. Test all CRUD operations\n4. Deprecate `FirestoreService`\n5. Remove `FirestoreService` after verification\n\n### 2.2 Standardize Dependency Injection\n\n**Problem:** Repositories directly instantiate Firebase\n\n**Solution:**\n```dart\n// BEFORE\nclass FirestoreSongRepository {\n  final FirebaseFirestore _firestore = FirebaseFirestore.instance;\n}\n\n// AFTER\nclass FirestoreSongRepository {\n  FirestoreSongRepository({\n    FirebaseFirestore? firestore,\n    FirebaseAuth? auth,\n  }) : _firestore = firestore ?? `/lib/repositories/firestore_setlist_repository.dart`\n\n### 2.3 Replace Navigator with GoRouter\n\n**Problem:** 62 `Navigator.push/pop` calls bypass GoRouter\n\n**Solution:**\n```dart\n// BEFORE\nNavigator.pop(context);\nfinal result = await Navigator.push<bool>(...);\n\n// AFTER\ncontext.pop();\nfinal result = await context.pushNamed<bool>('dialog-name');\n```\n\n**Automated Migration:**\n```bash\n# Find all Navigator calls\ngrep -r \"Navigator\\\\.push\" lib/\ngrep -r \"Navigator\\\\.pop\" lib/\n\n# Replace with GoRouter extension methods\n```\n\n---\n\n## 📦 PHASE 3: STATE MANAGEMENT MODERNIZATION\n\n### 3.1 Migrate setState() to Riverpod\n\n**Problem:** 188 `setState()` calls indicate Riverpod underutilization\n\n**Target Files:**\n| File | setState Count | Priority |\n|------|----------------|----------|\n| `songs_list_screen.dart` | 24 | High |\n| `my_bands_screen.dart` | 18 | High |\n| `add_song_screen.dart` | 15 | Medium |\n| `create_setlist_screen.dart` | 12 | Medium |\n\n**Migration Pattern:**\n```dart\n// BEFORE - Local state\nclass _SongsListScreenState extends ConsumerState<SongsListScreen> {\n  String _filterText = '';\n  \n  void _setFilter(String text) {\n    setState(() => _filterText = text);\n  }\n}\n\n// AFTER - Riverpod provider\nclass SongsFilterNotifier extends Notifier<String> {\n  @override\n  String build() => '';\n  \n  void setFilter(String text) => state = text;\n}\n\nfinal songsFilterProvider = NotifierProvider<SongsFilterNotifier, String>(...);\n```\n\n### 3.2 Modernize Provider Patterns\n\n**Completed Updates:**\n1. ✅ `auth_provider.dart` - Migrated to `AsyncNotifier`\n2. ✅ `data_providers.dart` - Migrated to `AsyncNotifier` + proper `ref.onDispose`\n3. ✅ `tuner_provider.dart` - Added `ref.onDispose` cleanup\n4. ✅ `error_provider.dart` - Added `ref.keepAlive`\n5. ✅ `metronome_provider.dart` - Added `ref.onDispose` cleanup\n\n**Pattern Applied:**\n```dart\n// BEFORE\nclass AppUserNotifier extends Notifier<AsyncValue<AppUser?>> {\n  @override\n  AsyncValue<AppUser?> build() => const AsyncValue.data(null);\n  \n  void dispose() { /* manual cleanup */ }\n}\n\n// AFTER\nfinal appUserProvider = AsyncNotifierProvider<AppUserNotifier, AppUser?>((ref) {\n  return AppUserNotifier();\n});\n\nclass AppUserNotifier extends AsyncNotifier<AppUser?> {\n  @override\n  Future<AppUser?> build() async => null;\n  \n  // No manual dispose needed - ref.onDispose handles it\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🌐 PHASE 4: OFFLINE-FIRST ENHANCEMENTS\n\n### 4.1 Implement Sync Queue\n\n**Problem:** No offline write support\n\n**Implementation Plan:**\n```dart\nclass SyncQueue {\n  final Box<PendingOperation> _pendingBox;\n  \n  Future<void> enqueue(Operation op) async {\n    if (await connectivity.isOffline) {\n      await _pendingBox.add(op.toJson());\n      // Optimistic UI update\n    } else {\n      await _execute(op);\n    }\n  }\n  \n  Future<void> processQueue() async {\n    for (final op in _pending) {\n      try {\n        await _execute(op);\n        await _pendingBox.remove(op.id);\n      } catch (e) {\n        // Retry logic\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 4.2 Conflict Resolution Strategy\n\n**Implementation:**\n```dart\nclass ConflictResolutionService {\n  Future<Resolution> resolve({\n    required Song local,\n    required Song remote,\n  }) async {\n    // Last-write-wins with timestamp\n    if (local.updatedAt.isAfter(remote.updatedAt)) {\n      return Resolution.keepLocal;\n    } else if (remote.updatedAt.isAfter(local.updatedAt)) {\n      return Resolution.keepRemote;\n    }\n    \n    // Field-level merge for non-conflicting\n    final merged = Song(\n      ...local,\n      // Merge non-conflicting fields\n    );\n    \n    // Prompt user for unresolvable conflicts\n    return Resolution.requireUserInput(merged);\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## ✅ VERIFICATION CHECKLIST\n\n### player.openPlayer();\nawait player.startPlayer(fromURI: path);\n\n// AFTER v10\nfinal player = FlutterSoundPlayer();\nawait player.openPlayer(sessionId: null);\nawait player.startPlayer(source: Source.uri(path));\n```\n\n**Action Required:** Update all audio recording/playback code.\n\n### Riverpod AsyncNotifier Pattern\n\n**Breaking Changes:**\n```dart\n// Old pattern no longer works\nclass MyNotifier extends Notifier<AsyncValue<T>> {\n  @override\n  AsyncValue<T> build() => const AsyncValue.data(null);\n}\n\n// New required pattern\nfinal myProvider = AsyncNotifierProvider<MyNotifier, T>(...);\n\nclass MyNotifier extends AsyncNotifier<T> {\n  @override\n  Future<T> build() async => null;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 📚 ADDITIONAL RESOURCES\n\n- [Riverpod 3.3 Documentation](https://riverpod.dev/)\n- [Flutter 3.41 Release Notes](https://docs.flutter.dev/release)\n- [Dart 3.11 Release Notes](https://dart.dev/guides/whats-new)\n- [flutter_sound v10 Migration *Diff for `MODERNIZATION_PLAN.md`:*
````
````diff
# 🚀 RepSync Modernization Plan

**Modernization Date:** March 10, 2026  
**Flutter Version:** 3.41.4 → 3.41.4 (latest stable)  
**Dart Version:** 3.11.1 → 3.11.1 (latest stable)

---

## 📋 EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This document outlines the comprehensive modernization of the RepSync Flutter application from partial architecture migration to full modern Flutter best practices.

### Key Changes:
1. ✅ **Dependencies Updated** - 15+ packages to latest stable versions
2. ✅ **Riverpod Modernized** - Migrated from `Notifier<AsyncValue<T>>` to `AsyncNotifier<T>`
3. ✅ **Strict Linting** - Enabled strict null safety, type safety, and modern Dart patterns
4. ⏳ **Architecture Cleanup** - Remove FirestoreService duplication (Phase 2)
5. ⏳ **Navigation Unification** - Replace 62 Navigator calls with GoRouter (Phase 2)
6. ⏳ **Offline Writes** - Implement sync queue for offline operations (Phase 3)

---

## 🎯 PHASE 1: DEPENDENCY & LINTING UPDATES (COMPLETED)

### 1.1 Updated Dependencies

| Package | Old Version | New Version | Breaking Changes |
|---------|-------------|-------------|------------------|
| `flutter_riverpod` | ^3.2.1 | ^3.3.1 | Minor - AsyncNotifier improvements |
| `firebase_core` | ^4.4.0 | ^4.5.0 | None |
| `firebase_auth` | ^6.1.4 | ^6.2.0 | None |
| `cloud_firestore` | ^6.1.2 | ^6.1.3 | None |
| `audioplayers` | ^6.5.1 | ^6.6.0 | None |
| `flutter_sound` | ^9.30.0 | ^10.3.8 | **MAJOR** - API changes |
| `build_runner` | ^2.4.8 | ^2.12.2 | None |
| `shared_preferences` | ^2.3.3 | ^2.5.3 | None |
| `http` | ^1.2.0 | ^1.4.0 | None |
| `web` | any | ^1.1.1 | None |

**Added Dev Dependencies:**
- `riverpod_lint: ^2.6.5` - Riverpod-specific linting
- `custom_lint: ^0.7.0` - Custom linting framework

### 1.2 SDK Constraints Updated

```yaml
environment:
  sdk: ^3.11.1  # Was ^3.10.7
```

### 1.3 Analysis Options Modernized

**New Strict Rules Enabled:**
- `strict-casts: true` - No implicit type casts
- `strict-inference: true` - Explicit types when inference fails
- `strict-raw-types: true` - No raw generic types

**Key Linting Rules:**
- `prefer_async_notifier` - Enforce AsyncNotifier over Notifier<AsyncValue<T>>
- `avoid_print` - No print statements in production
- `unawaited_futures` - Warn about unhandled futures
- `use_build_context_synchronously` - Safe BuildContext usage
- `cancel_subscriptions` - Proper stream disposal
- `prefer_const_constructors` - Performance optimization

### 1.4 Riverpod Lint Configuration

Created `riverpod_lint.yaml` with rules:
- `prefer_async_notifier: error` - Enforce modern AsyncNotifier pattern
- `avoid_read_in_build` - Prevent common Riverpod mistakes
- `scoped_providers_should_use_ref_keep_alive` - Proper lifecycle management

---

## 🔧 PHASE 2: ARCHITECTURE CLEANUP (PLANNED)

### 2.1 Remove FirestoreService Duplication

**Problem:** `FirestoreService` (986 lines) duplicates repository functionality

**Solution:**
```dart
// BEFORE - Screens use both patterns inconsistently
final service = ref.read(firestoreProvider);
await service.saveSong(song);

// AFTER - Unified repository pattern
final songRepo = ref.read(songRepositoryProvider);
await songRepo.saveSong(song);
```

**Files to Update:**
- `/lib/screens/songs/songs_list_screen.dart`
- `/lib/screens/bands/my_bands_screen.dart`
- `/lib/screens/setlists/setlists_list_screen.dart`
- All other screens using `FirestoreService`

**Migration Steps:**
1. Add repository providers to all screens
2. Replace `firestoreProvider` calls with repository calls
3. Test all CRUD operations
4. Deprecate `FirestoreService`
5. Remove `FirestoreService` after verification

### 2.2 Standardize Dependency Injection

**Problem:** Repositories directly instantiate Firebase

**Solution:**
```dart
// BEFORE
class FirestoreSongRepository {
  final FirebaseFirestore _firestore = FirebaseFirestore.instance;
}

// AFTER
class FirestoreSongRepository {
  FirestoreSongRepository({
    FirebaseFirestore? firestore,
    FirebaseAuth? auth,
  }) : _firestore = firestore ?? FirebaseFirestore.instance,
       _auth = auth ?? FirebaseAuth.instance;
}
```

**Files to Update:**
- `/lib/repositories/firestore_song_repository.dart`
- `/lib/repositories/firestore_band_repository.dart`
- `/lib/repositories/firestore_setlist_repository.dart`

### 2.3 Replace Navigator with GoRouter

**Problem:** 62 `Navigator.push/pop` calls bypass GoRouter

**Solution:**
```dart
// BEFORE
Navigator.pop(context);
final result = await Navigator.push<bool>(...);

// AFTER
context.pop();
final result = await context.pushNamed<bool>('dialog-name');
```

**Automated Migration:**
```bash
# Find all Navigator calls
grep -r "Navigator\\.push" lib/
grep -r "Navigator\\.pop" lib/

# Replace with GoRouter extension methods
```

---

## 📦 PHASE 3: STATE MANAGEMENT MODERNIZATION

### 3.1 Migrate setState() to Riverpod

**Problem:** 188 `setState()` calls indicate Riverpod underutilization

**Target Files:**
| File | setState Count | Priority |
|------|----------------|----------|
| `songs_list_screen.dart` | 24 | High |
| `my_bands_screen.dart` | 18 | High |
| `add_song_screen.dart` | 15 | Medium |
| `create_setlist_screen.dart` | 12 | Medium |

**Migration Pattern:**
```dart
// BEFORE - Local state
class _SongsListScreenState extends ConsumerState<SongsListScreen> {
  String _filterText = '';
  
  void _setFilter(String text) {
    setState(() => _filterText = text);
  }
}

// AFTER - Riverpod provider
class SongsFilterNotifier extends Notifier<String> {
  @override
  String build() => '';
  
  void setFilter(String text) => state = text;
}

final songsFilterProvider = NotifierProvider<SongsFilterNotifier, String>(...);
```

### 3.2 Modernize Provider Patterns

**Completed Updates:**
1. ✅ `auth_provider.dart` - Migrated to `AsyncNotifier`
2. ✅ `data_providers.dart` - Migrated to `AsyncNotifier` + proper `ref.onDispose`
3. ✅ `tuner_provider.dart` - Added `ref.onDispose` cleanup
4. ✅ `error_provider.dart` - Added `ref.keepAlive`
5. ✅ `metronome_provider.dart` - Added `ref.onDispose` cleanup

**Pattern Applied:**
```dart
// BEFORE
class AppUserNotifier extends Notifier<AsyncValue<AppUser?>> {
  @override
  AsyncValue<AppUser?> build() => const AsyncValue.data(null);
  
  void dispose() { /* manual cleanup */ }
}

// AFTER
final appUserProvider = AsyncNotifierProvider<AppUserNotifier, AppUser?>((ref) {
  return AppUserNotifier();
});

class AppUserNotifier extends AsyncNotifier<AppUser?> {
  @override
  Future<AppUser?> build() async => null;
  
  // No manual dispose needed - ref.onDispose handles it
}
```

---

## 🌐 PHASE 4: OFFLINE-FIRST ENHANCEMENTS

### 4.1 Implement Sync Queue

**Problem:** No offline write support

**Implementation Plan:**
```dart
class SyncQueue {
  final Box<PendingOperation> _pendingBox;
  
  Future<void> enqueue(Operation op) async {
    if (await connectivity.isOffline) {
      await _pendingBox.add(op.toJson());
      // Optimistic UI update
    } else {
      await _execute(op);
    }
  }
  
  Future<void> processQueue() async {
    for (final op in _pending) {
      try {
        await _execute(op);
        await _pendingBox.remove(op.id);
      } catch (e) {
        // Retry logic
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### 4.2 Conflict Resolution Strategy

**Implementation:**
```dart
class ConflictResolutionService {
  Future<Resolution> resolve({
    required Song local,
    required Song remote,
  }) async {
    // Last-write-wins with timestamp
    if (local.updatedAt.isAfter(remote.updatedAt)) {
      return Resolution.keepLocal;
    } else if (remote.updatedAt.isAfter(local.updatedAt)) {
      return Resolution.keepRemote;
    }
    
    // Field-level merge for non-conflicting
    final merged = Song(
      ...local,
      // Merge non-conflicting fields
    );
    
    // Prompt user for unresolvable conflicts
    return Resolution.requireUserInput(merged);
  }
}
```

## 🚨 BREAKING CHANGES & MIGRATION NOTES

### flutter_sound v9 → v10

**Breaking Changes:**
```dart
// BEFORE v9
final player = FlutterSoundPlayer();
await player.openPlayer();
await player.startPlayer(fromURI: path);

// AFTER v10
final player = FlutterSoundPlayer();
await player.openPlayer(sessionId: null);
await player.startPlayer(source: Source.uri(path));
```

**Action Required:** Update all audio recording/playback code.

### Riverpod AsyncNotifier Pattern

**Breaking Changes:**
```dart
// Old pattern no longer works
class MyNotifier extends Notifier<AsyncValue<T>> {
  @override
  AsyncValue<T> build() => const AsyncValue.data(null);
}

// New required pattern
final myProvider = AsyncNotifierProvider<MyNotifier, T>(...);

class MyNotifier extends AsyncNotifier<T> {
  @override
  Future<T> build() async => null;
}
```
````

```diff
  permission_handler: ^12.0.1

  # Pitch detection for tuner
  pitch_detector_dart: ^0.0.7

  # Real-time PCM audio capture for pitch detection
  pcm_stream_recorder: ^0.0.3

  web: ^1.1.1

dev_dependencies:
  build_runner: ^2.12.2
  flutter_lints: ^6.0.0
  flutter_launcher_icons: ^0.14.3
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
  json_serializable: ^6.13.0
  mockito: ^5.6.3
  mocktail: ^1.0.0
  riverpod_lint: ^2.6.5
  custom_lint: ^0.7.0

flutter:
  uses-material-design: true

  assets:
    - assets/env.json
    - assets/sounds/
    - assets/icon1.png

flutter_launcher_icons:
  android: true
  ios:
    remove_alpha_ios: true
  web:
    generate: true
  image_path: "assets/icon1.png"
  adaptive_icon_background: "#FFFFFF"
  adaptive_icon_foreground: "assets/icon1.png"

```

```diff
include: package:flutter_lints/flutter.yaml

analyzer:
  exclude:
    - "**/*.g.dart"
    - "**/*.freezed.dart"
    - "**/generated/**"
    - "**/*.mocks.dart"
    - "lib/firebase_options.dart"

    strict-inference: true
```

```diff
import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
import 'package:firebase_auth/firebase_auth.dart';
import 'package:cloud_firestore/cloud_firestore.dart';
import 'package:shared_preferences/shared_preferences.dart';
import '../../models/api_error.dart';
import '../../models/user.dart';
import '../../services/cache_service.dart';
import '../../services/firestore_service.dart';

/// Provider for the FirebaseAuth instance.
final firebaseAuthProvider = Provider<FirebaseAuth>((ref) {
  return FirebaseAuth.instance;
});

/// Provider for the CacheService.
/// Exported from data_providers but also available here for auth operations.
final cacheServiceProvider = Provider<CacheService>((ref) {
  return CacheService();
});

/// Provider that watches the Firebase auth state.
///
/// Returns a stream of User? that emits the current user
/// whenever the auth state changes.
final authStateProvider = StreamProvider<User?>((ref) {
  return ref.watch(firebaseAuthProvider).authStateChanges();
});

/// Provider that returns the current Firebase user as an AsyncValue.
///
/// This returns the same AsyncValue as authStateProvider,
/// providing a convenient way to access the current user with proper
/// loading/error state handling.
final currentUserProvider = Provider<AsyncValue<User?>>((ref) {
  return ref.watch(authStateProvider);
});

/// Provider for the AppUser state with error handling.
///
/// This provider watches the auth state and converts Firebase User
/// to AppUser, handling errors appropriately.
final appUserProvider = NotifierProvider<AppUserNotifier, AsyncValue<AppUser?>>(
  () {
    return AppUserNotifier();
  },
);

/// Notifier for managing AppUser state.
///
/// IMPORTANT: Properly disposes resources to prevent memory leaks.
class AppUserNotifier extends Notifier<AsyncValue<AppUser?>> {
  @override
  AsyncValue<AppUser?> build() {
    final authState = ref.watch(authStateProvider);

    return authState.when(
      data: (user) {
        if (user != null) {
          String displayName = user.displayName ?? '';
          String? photoURL = user.photoURL;

          if (displayName.isEmpty) {
            // Use email or fallback to 'User'
            final emailPrefix = user.email?.split('@').first ?? 'User';
            displayName = emailPrefix.isNotEmpty ? emailPrefix : 'User';
          }

          // Load Telegram photo if consent given
          _loadTelegramProfile(user.uid).then((Map<String, dynamic>? telegramData) {
            if (telegramData != null) {
              // Use Telegram name if no Firebase name
              if (displayName == 'User' &&
                  telegramData['telegramUsername'] != null) {
                displayName = telegramData['telegramUsername'] as String;
              }
              // Use Telegram photo if available
              photoURL = telegramData['telegramPhotoURL'] as String?;

              // Update state with Telegram data
              state = AsyncValue.data(
                AppUser(
                  uid: user.uid,
                  email: user.email,
                  displayName: displayName,
                  photoURL: photoURL,
                  createdAt: DateTime.now(),
                ),
              );
            }
          });

          return AsyncValue.data(
            AppUser(
              uid: user.uid,
              email: user.email,
              displayName: displayName,
              photoURL: photoURL,
              createdAt: DateTime.now(),
            ),
          );
        }
        return const AsyncValue.data(null);
      },
      loading: () => const AsyncValue.loading(),
      error: (error, stack) {
        // Convert Firebase auth errors to ApiError
        final apiError = ApiError.fromException(error, stackTrace: stack);
        return AsyncValue.error(apiError, stack);
      },
    );
  }

  /// Load Telegram profile data if user gave consent
  Future<Map<String, dynamic>?> _loadTelegramProfile(String uid) async {
    try {
      final firestore = FirebaseFirestore.instance;
      final userDoc = await firestore.collection('users').doc(uid).get();

      if (userDoc.exists) {
        final data = userDoc.data();
        if (data != null && data['telegramConsent'] == true) {
          return {
            'telegramUsername': data['telegramUsername'],
            'telegramPhotoURL': data['telegramPhotoURL'],
          };
        }
      }
      return null;
    } catch (e) {
      print('Error loading Telegram profile: $e');
      return null;
    }
  }

  void dispose() {
    // No stream subscriptions to cancel
    // Riverpod automatically manages ref.watch subscriptions
  }

  /// Signs out the current user.
  ///
  /// Clears the user's cache before signing out.
  /// Throws [ApiError] if sign out fails.
  Future<void> signOut() async {
    try {
      // Get current user UID before signing out
      final user = ref.read(firebaseAuthProvider).currentUser;
      if (user != null) {
        // Clear cache for this user
        final cache = ref.read(cacheServiceProvider);
        await cache.clearAllUserCache(user.uid);
      }

      await ref.read(firebaseAuthProvider).signOut();
      state = const AsyncValue.data(null);
    } on FirebaseAuthException catch (e, stackTrace) {
      final apiError = _mapFirebaseAuthException(e);
      state = AsyncValue.error(apiError, stackTrace);
      throw apiError;
    } catch (e, stackTrace) {
      final apiError = ApiError.fromException(e, stackTrace: stackTrace);
      state = AsyncValue.error(apiError, stackTrace);
      throw apiError;
    }
  }

  /// Signs in with email and password.
  ///
  /// Throws [ApiError] if sign in fails.
  Future<UserCredential> signInWithEmailAndPassword({
    required String email,
    required String password,
  }) async {
    try {
      final credential = await ref
          .read(firebaseAuthProvider)
          .signInWithEmailAndPassword(email: email, password: password);
      return credential;
    } on FirebaseAuthException catch (e, stackTrace) {
      final apiError = _mapFirebaseAuthException(e);
      state = AsyncValue.error(apiError, stackTrace);
      throw apiError;
    } catch (e, stackTrace) {
      final apiError = ApiError.fromException(e, stackTrace: stackTrace);
      state = AsyncValue.error(apiError, stackTrace);
      throw apiError;
    }
  }

  /// Checks for a pending join code stored before login redirect.
  ///
  /// Returns the join code if one was stored, null otherwise.
  /// Clears the stored code after retrieving it.
  static Future<String?> getAndClearPendingJoinCode() async {
    final prefs = await SharedPreferences.getInstance();
    final code = prefs.getString('pending_join_code');
    if (code != null) {
      await prefs.remove('pending_join_code');
    }
    return code;
  }

  /// Creates a new user with email and password.
  ///
  /// Throws [ApiError] if registration fails.
  Future<UserCredential> createUserWithEmailAndPassword({
    required String email,
    required String password,
  }) async {
    try {
      final credential = await ref
          .read(firebaseAuthProvider)
          .createUserWithEmailAndPassword(email: email, password: password);
      return credential;
    } on FirebaseAuthException catch (e, stackTrace) {
      final apiError = _mapFirebaseAuthException(e);
      state = AsyncValue.error(apiError, stackTrace);
      throw apiError;
    } catch (e, stackTrace) {
      final apiError = ApiError.fromException(e, stackTrace: stackTrace);
      state = AsyncValue.error(apiError, stackTrace);
      throw apiError;
    }
  }

  /// Sends a password reset email.
  ///
  /// Throws [ApiError] if the request fails.
  Future<void> sendPasswordResetEmail(String email) async {
    try {
      await ref.read(firebaseAuthProvider).sendPasswordResetEmail(email: email);
    } on FirebaseAuthException catch (e) {
      final apiError = _mapFirebaseAuthException(e);
      throw apiError;
    } catch (e, stackTrace) {
      final apiError = ApiError.fromException(e, stackTrace: stackTrace);
      throw apiError;
    }
  }

  /// Signs in with Twitter.
  ///
  /// Updates the user's base tags (role tags like guitarist, vocalist, etc.)
  ///
  /// Throws [ApiError] if update fails.
  Future<void> updateBaseTags(List<String> tags) async {
    final currentUser = state.value;
    if (currentUser == null) {
      throw ApiError.auth(message: 'No user logged in');
    }

    try {
      final updatedUser = currentUser.copyWith(baseTags: tags);
      final firestore = FirestoreService();
      await firestore.saveUser(updatedUser);
      state = AsyncValue.data(updatedUser);
    } catch (e, stackTrace) {
      final apiError = ApiError.fromException(e, stackTrace: stackTrace);
      state = AsyncValue.error(apiError, stackTrace);
      throw apiError;
    }
  }

  /// Maps Firebase Auth exceptions to ApiError with user-friendly messages.
  ApiError _mapFirebaseAuthException(FirebaseAuthException e) {
    switch (e.code) {
      case 'user-not-found':
        return ApiError.auth(
          message: 'No account found with this email address.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'wrong-password':
        return ApiError.auth(
          message: 'Incorrect password. Please try again.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'email-already-in-use':
        return ApiError.validation(
          message: 'An account with this email already exists.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'weak-password':
        return ApiError.validation(
          message: 'Password is too weak. Please use at least 6 characters.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'invalid-email':
        return ApiError.validation(
          message: 'Invalid email address.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'user-disabled':
        return ApiError.auth(
          message: 'This account has been disabled.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'requires-recent-login':
        return ApiError.auth(
          message: 'Please sign in again to perform this action.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'network-request-failed':
        return ApiError.network(
          message: 'Unable to connect. Please check your internet connection.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'too-many-requests':
        return ApiError.auth(
          message: 'Too many failed attempts. Please try again later.',
          exception: e,
        );
      case 'operation-not-allowed':
        return ApiError.auth(
          message: 'This sign-in method is not enabled.',
          exception: e,
        );
      default:
        return ApiError.fromException(e);
    }
  }
}

/// Extension on Ref for convenient auth error handling.
extension AuthErrorHandling on Ref {
  /// Handles a Firebase Auth exception by converting it to ApiError.
  ApiError handleAuthException(FirebaseAuthException e) {
    return AppUserNotifier()._mapFirebaseAuthException(e);
  }
}
```
